description_long: This shelter features a compact, elevated structure with a small platform accessed by a set of stairs. The main body is constructed from a combination of wood and metal, with a partially enclosed seating area at the top, protected by a flat, overhanging roof. The shelter is supported by sturdy vertical posts and includes a railing for safety along the stairs and platform. The design is utilitarian, likely intended for use as a lookout or lifeguard station in urban parks or recreational areas. The base is set on a striped, hard-surfaced platform, suggesting installation in a public or semi-public space. The overall appearance is functional, with minimal decorative elements and a focus on durability and weather protection.
description: A compact, elevated shelter with stairs, a small platform, and a flat overhanging roof, constructed from wood and metal. Designed for functional use in public spaces, it features safety railings and a utilitarian aesthetic.
description_view_0: Front view showing the stairs leading up to the platform and the open entryway beneath the flat roof.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the vertical support posts, the railing, and the depth of the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the shelter, the enclosed seating area, and the structure of the platform.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the stairs from a different angle and the open nature of the entryway.
attribute_roof_type: flat wooden slab roof with slight overhang
attribute_entryway_design: open entry with stairs and safety railing
attribute_support_structure: vertical wooden posts and metal railings
